macro_events.event_summary: Trump threatens escalated military strikes on Iran if no peace/nuclear deal is reached, raising Middle East conflict risk premium.
primary_drivers: Geopolitical risk premium on crude oil from potential Strait of Hormuz / Persian Gulf supply disruption
Defense spending and munitions demand expectations rise on escalation rhetoric
Safe-haven flows into gold and defensive assets

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US defense prime with direct exposure to missile defense (THAAD, PAC-3 Patriot) — systems already deployed regionally and replenished after prior Iran/Houthi engagements. Direct munitions reorder beneficiary on escalation.
Makes Patriot interceptors, Tomahawks, and SM-series missiles — the exact munitions consumed in CENTCOM operations against Iran-aligned forces. Replenishment contracts follow strike activity.
Leidos and defense IT/services names see contract flow on heightened CENTCOM ops tempo; cleaner pure-play than diversified primes for sentiment trade.
Largest US integrated oil — direct beneficiary of Brent risk premium. Limited Iran/Gulf upstream exposure means upside without offsetting asset risk if conflict escalates.
High-beta US shale producer with Permian-weighted production; earnings leverage to crude price spike is among the highest in large-cap E&P.
Kratos drones and target systems — small-cap defense name with outsized momentum response to conflict headlines; tactical missile and unmanned systems exposure.

losers.rationale: United has the most international long-haul exposure among US carriers including routes that overfly or near Middle East airspace; jet fuel cost spike on crude rally compresses margins directly.
Delta similarly exposed to jet fuel cost passthrough; transatlantic and codeshare routes face rerouting costs if Gulf airspace risk rises.
Carnival cruise itineraries include Mediterranean/Middle East; bunker fuel costs rise with crude and demand softens on regional travel risk perception.
Global air freight network is fuel-cost sensitive and Middle East routing matters; jet fuel is a major variable cost with imperfect surcharge passthrough on contract freight.
Commodity chemicals — naphtha/ethane feedstock costs rise with crude while pricing power into a soft demand backdrop is limited; margin squeeze on crude spikes is well-established.

headlines: Trump says Iran will be bombed 'at much higher level' if they don't agree to peace deal

macro_events.event_summary: Mortgage rates climbed to a one-month high, pushing first-time and lower-income homebuyers out of the purchase market.
primary_drivers: Higher mortgage rates reduce affordability and purchase mortgage demand
Reduced first-time buyer activity shifts demand toward rentals and existing-home turnover slows
Negative read-through to homebuilder volumes and mortgage origination revenue

beneficiaries.rationale: Single-family rental REIT — when first-time buyers are priced out of purchase, they remain renters, supporting occupancy and rent growth in SFR portfolios.
American Homes 4 Rent: same SFR thesis as INVH; lower-income/first-time buyer dropout directly extends rental demand in their core demographic.
Multifamily apartment REIT benefits at the margin as the buy-vs-rent calculation tilts further toward renting at higher mortgage rates.

losers.rationale: D.R. Horton has the highest exposure to entry-level/first-time buyers among large builders (Express brand); first-time buyer dropout directly hits order volumes and forces incentive/rate-buydown spending that compresses margins.
Lennar is similarly skewed to entry-level product; rising rates force deeper mortgage rate-buydown incentives, a direct gross margin headwind already flagged in recent guidance.
KB Home is a near-pure-play first-time buyer builder in CA/TX/AZ — the exact cohort cited as dropping out. Highest beta to this headline among builders.
Rocket Companies' revenue is dominated by purchase and refi origination volume; higher rates directly suppress application volume and gain-on-sale margins.
UWM Holdings is a pure-play wholesale mortgage originator — origination volumes fall mechanically with higher rates and lower purchase activity.
Zillow's revenue tied to housing transaction volume and Premier Agent spend; lower first-time buyer activity reduces lead value and transaction throughput.
Opendoor's iBuying model depends on transaction velocity and stable home prices; affordability-driven demand drop raises holding-period risk on inventory.
Builders FirstSource sells lumber/structural products into single-family new construction — builder volume softness flows directly into their revenue.

headlines: Mortgage rates hit the highest level in a month, causing first-time homebuyers to drop out

macro_events.event_summary: Gold and silver prices jumped on a report that the US and Iran are nearing a peace deal, suggesting potential easing of Middle East tensions and possible Iranian oil supply returning to global markets.
primary_drivers: Potential return of ~1-1.5mbpd Iranian oil exports lowering crude prices
Reduced Middle East geopolitical risk premium
Safe-haven flows paradox: gold/silver rising on the headline likely reflects USD weakness/dovish read rather than risk-off

beneficiaries.rationale: Airlines are direct beneficiaries of lower jet fuel costs if Iranian crude returns to market; fuel is ~25-30% of operating costs and Delta has high domestic/international exposure with limited hedging.
Southwest historically under-hedged on fuel; pure-play domestic carrier with high beta to crude price declines. Lower oil from Iranian supply return flows directly to margins.
Pan American Silver is a leveraged silver producer; with silver spiking on the headline (dovish/USD-weakness read), miners get operating leverage on each $1 move in silver price.
Newmont is the largest pure-play gold miner; gold price jump translates to direct margin expansion given relatively fixed AISC around $1,400/oz.
Cruise lines are bunker fuel-intensive and Middle East detente reduces both fuel cost and itinerary risk for Mediterranean/Red Sea routes.

losers.rationale: Exxon's upstream earnings are highly leveraged to Brent; a return of Iranian barrels (~1mbpd+) to legal markets would pressure global oil prices and compress E&P margins.
Occidental is a high-beta domestic E&P with significant Permian leverage; oil price decline from Iranian supply return hits cash flow disproportionately given its leverage.
Lockheed Martin's missile defense (PAC-3, THAAD) and F-35 demand carries a Middle East tension premium; peace deal removes near-term FMS urgency from Gulf states.
Raytheon's missile and air defense systems benefit from Iran-related procurement; reduced regional tension softens the demand narrative even if backlog remains.
APA Corp has direct Egypt/offshore exposure and high oil-price beta; lower Brent from Iranian supply hits both realized prices and exploration economics.

headlines: Gold, silver jump on report that the US and Iran are nearing a peace deal

macro_events.event_summary: A Chinese-government-linked study warns that the EU's plan to phase out Chinese technology (telecom, cloud, EV components, solar, etc.) would cost the bloc over $400 billion, signaling escalation of EU-China tech decoupling.
primary_drivers: EU-China tech decoupling creates substitution demand for non-Chinese vendors
Higher capex/cost burden on European telecom and industrial buyers
Reduced China revenue exposure risk for Western firms with European footprints

beneficiaries.rationale: First Solar is a non-Chinese solar module manufacturer; EU phase-out of Chinese solar panels (Longi, JinkoSolar, Trina) would redirect European utility-scale demand to First Solar's thin-film tech, which it is actively expanding capacity for.

STMicroelectronics is a European-domiciled chipmaker that would gain share in EV power semis and industrial chips if Chinese suppliers (e.g., SMIC-linked) are phased out of European auto and industrial supply chains.

If EU mirrors US restrictions on Chinese AI/cloud tech (Alibaba Cloud, Huawei Ascend), European hyperscalers and sovereign AI buildouts shift incremental GPU demand to Nvidia rather than Chinese accelerators.

losers.rationale: Apple has heavy China manufacturing exposure (Foxconn, Luxshare); escalating EU-China tech war raises tail risk of Chinese retaliation against Western tech firms operating in China, where Apple derives ~19% of revenue.

Tesla's Shanghai Gigafactory exports to Europe; broader EU-China tech decoupling raises risk of reciprocal Chinese measures and complicates Tesla's bidirectional supply chain (CATL batteries into Europe).
Qualcomm derives over 60% of revenue from China; any escalation in EU-China decoupling raises probability of Chinese retaliation against US chip firms as a signal to Brussels.

headlines: EU plan to phase out Chinese tech could cost bloc over $400 billion, Chinese study says

macro_events.event_summary: Analysis piece framing an Iran war-driven oil price shock and its potential macroeconomic impact (higher crude, inflation pass-through, growth drag).
primary_drivers: Higher crude oil prices boosting upstream producer margins
Energy cost pass-through pressuring fuel-intensive consumer/industrial businesses
Geopolitical risk premium driving defense spending expectations and safe-haven flows

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with significant upstream exposure; every $10/bbl move in Brent flows directly to operating cash flow. Limited Iran/Strait of Hormuz operational risk vs. pure Middle East names.
Same upstream leverage as XOM with heavy Permian and Gulf of Mexico production — benefits from price without direct Persian Gulf asset risk.
Pure-play US shale E&P with no international Middle East exposure; near 100% of earnings tied to oil price realizations, making it a clean long on oil shock.
High-beta domestic E&P (Permian) with leveraged balance sheet — historically the largest equity gainer per dollar of crude upside in shock scenarios.
Iran war framing drives expectations of accelerated DoD munitions/missile defense procurement (PAC-3, THAAD); LMT is the prime contractor for both.
Raytheon makes the SM-series and Patriot interceptors actively used in regional missile defense; replenishment orders likely if hostilities escalate.
Oilfield services leverage to higher rig counts as $90+ Brent incentivizes US shale activity reacceleration.
Permian pure-play with high oil cut and shareholder return framework that scales variable dividends with crude price.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating costs and hedging is limited; an oil shock compresses margins immediately and demand destruction risk if fares rise.
Same fuel cost mechanic as DAL with higher international exposure; Middle East airspace closures add re-routing costs.
Domestic carrier with historically strong fuel hedging program now largely rolled off; unhedged exposure to crude spike hits earnings directly.
Bunker fuel is a top-3 cost line for cruise operators and discretionary travel demand is squeezed by higher gasoline prices at the consumer level.
Jet/diesel fuel is a major cost; while surcharges pass through with a lag, the margin compression in the interim period is meaningful.
Low-income consumer base is most sensitive to gasoline prices — every $0.50/gal at the pump directly reduces discretionary basket spend at dollar stores.
Petrochemical feedstock costs (naphtha/ethane) rise with crude; commodity chemicals have limited ability to pass through in soft demand environment.

Counterintuitive — oil shock historically helps EV demand, BUT Tesla's auto margins and consumer financing demand are hurt more by the rate/inflation shock that follows oil spikes than helped by the gasoline-substitution narrative at current EV penetration levels.

headlines: 3 charts explain how the Iran war oil shock could impact the economy
